film.title: Billoo Baadshah
film.overview: An orphan makes sacrifices for the family who took him in but subsequently faces humiliation and homelessness.

dialogues.dialogue: In the sight of humanity, you are not Billu, the rickshaw driver....you are Billu Badshah. Accept this small gift from me, Mr. Tiwari. What a shame. No, Mr. Gosangi. Don't entrap me in this trap of money. I don't want it. Take it away.

I'll say it. I'll say it thousands of times. Why should I be afraid of her? Shameless. You should have taken a poison before you said this. Why should I consume poison? Let her consume it if she wants.
Stop talking nonsense. Asha. Asha. Shameless girl. What have you done? She had said it for your own good. Don't shout at me, Mother.
Let's give this food to him. He'll eat it and he'll like the taste. What are you saying? You should feel ashamed. Will you serve tasted food to brother? I didn't know that you are so narrow-minded.
dialogues.cause_raw: serving tasted food to a guest

dialogues.dialogue: What do you want? - Don't act innocent. Tell me, Sister-in-law. Why don't you wash your clothes yourself? You should feel ashamed. Come on, wash your clothes right now. My foot! I'm not used to do such trivial jobs.
They've gone to Delhi. - What? Mummy-daddy gone, very good. Have some shame, shameless. Those who hesitate are doomed. You know, darling, there's no censor between husband and wife.

But Roshanlal will never accept a poor girl as a family member. Stop it, sir. Don't challenge my goodness, and my pride. Billu is not anybody's slave. I do everything as per my wishes. I'm the emperor of my heart.
Bravo, Ravi. For the first time you've acted like a man. Today, I can proudly say that you're my brother-in-law. Stop this 'brother-in-law' nonsense. Are you in your senses?
